claim_text: SWE-ContextBench extends SWE-Bench Lite into a context-learning benchmark by pairing 300 base tasks with 99 manually verified related tasks drawn from real issue and pull-request relationships.
caveats: No contradiction is visible in the benchmark-construction evidence.
mechanism_or_bounds: The benchmark’s context-learning structure comes from linking real-world base tasks to related tasks and validating those links, but this evidence supports the dataset design rather than any causal claim about agent performance.
contradiction_note: No contradiction is visible in the benchmark-construction evidence.

claim_text: SWE-ContextBench evaluates coding agents not just on task resolution but also on runtime and monetary cost using test-based outcome checks on related tasks.
caveats: No contradiction is visible in the evaluation-design evidence.
mechanism_or_bounds: The benchmark operationalizes evaluation through FAIL_TO_PASS and PASS_TO_PASS tests plus runtime and cost tracking; this establishes what is measured, but not that these measures fully capture real-world coding utility.
contradiction_note: No contradiction is visible in the evaluation-design evidence.

claim_text: In the reported results, oracle summary reuse outperformed both no-experience and oracle full-experience reuse, reaching 34.34% resolution versus 26.26% for the baseline and 27.27% for full trajectory reuse.
caveats: There is visible tension because fuller past experience did not yield comparable gains, which cuts against a simple more-context-is-better expectation.
mechanism_or_bounds: A plausible bound is that short curated summaries preserve the most decision-relevant context while avoiding the overhead of very long trajectories, but the paper evidence here does not prove that mechanism directly.
contradiction_note: There is visible tension because fuller past experience did not yield comparable gains, which cuts against a simple more-context-is-better expectation.

claim_text: The benchmark results suggest that summary quality matters, because free summary reuse performed worse than the no-experience baseline and produced the most patch application failures.
caveats: This result creates clear tension with any blanket claim that adding summaries is inherently beneficial.
mechanism_or_bounds: The bounded mechanism suggested by the evidence is that compressed context helps only when the summary is reliable and task-relevant; poor summaries may mislead the agent or degrade patch generation, but the exact failure pathway remains uncertain.
contradiction_note: This result creates clear tension with any blanket claim that adding summaries is inherently beneficial.
